channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Travis Kelce's best friend dishes on Taylor Swift's influence since two stars began dating
item.description: One of Travis Kelce's best friends talked to Page Six about Taylor Swift and her influence on him and around the group. The two started dating last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Augusta chairman confident Masters will go on as club focuses on community recovery from Helene
item.description: Masters chairman Fred Ridley says Augusta National sustained a lot of damage from Hurricane Helene, just like the rest of the area.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Simone Biles' post-Olympic tour is helping give men's gymnastics a post-Olympic boost
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Simone Biles simply wanted to mix it up when the gymnastics superstar invited some of the top American men to join her post-Olympic Tour. “Bringing the guys on board was designed to show what men’s gymnastics has to offer,” Biles said.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: PFF adds an in-game grading feature to its NFL analysis
item.description: Pro Football Focus changed how many fans followed the NFL by providing grades and advanced statistics to help quantify how every player performed on a play-by-play basis from high-profile quarterbacks like Patrick Mahomes to more anonymous guards and linebackers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Golf legend John Daly reveals Florida home destroyed in Helene: 'The memories is what you miss'
item.description: PGA Tour icon John Daly revealed his home in Clearwater, Florida, was destroyed in Hurricane Helene last week. He said he was glad everyone was OK.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caitlin Clark thanks supporters in heartwarming message: 'I am filled with gratitude'
item.description: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark capped off her rookie season with a message to her fans and supporters on Wednesday night. She had a wild 11 months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Picks of the Week: Washington, Cal and Arizona State well-positioned to cover (and win) in Week 6
item.description: Cal has several advantages against unbeaten Miami, including a familiarity with Canes coach Mario Cristobal.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Steve Kerr opens up on championship-or-bust culture: ‘This is not a zero-sum game’
item.description: In sharing his opinion on Ringz Culture, the head coach at least partially revealed his thoughts on the Warriors' roster.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Broncos out to snap 8-game losing streak to Raiders who have long overcome distractions vs. Denver
item.description: ENGLEWOOD, Colo. (AP) — A year after ending an ignominious losing streak against the Kansas City Chiefs, the Denver Broncos are out to extinguish another embarrassing skid Sunday when they host the Las Vegas Raiders.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ravens' Lamar Jackson doubles down on message to sports bettors: ‘We’re not worried about that’
item.description: Baltimore Ravens quarterback Lamar Jackson made it clear to sports bettors this week that the goal is winning games, not crushing stats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hall of Famer Susie Maxwell Berning, a 4-time major champion while raising 2 daughters, dies at 83
item.description: PALM SPRINGS, Calif. (AP) — Susie Maxwell Berning, the three-time U.S. Women's Open winner who was a pioneer as a mother while competing on the LPGA Tour, died Wednesday after a two-year battle with lung cancer. She was 83.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle Higashioka's solo homer started a five-run rally against an ailing Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 victory in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri coach Eli Drinkwitz revokes football staffer's access due to brother playing for upcoming opponent
item.description: As Missouri continues to prepare for an SEC showdown against Texas A&M, one member of the Tigers football staff has been locked out of practice sessions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lionel Messi, Inter Miami win MLS Supporters Shield with 3-2 win at Crew. It's Messi's 46th trophy.
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Lionel Messi and Inter Miami have another trophy, along with home-field advantage throughout the Major League Soccer playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Astros star posts cryptic 3-word comment after possible last game with Houston
item.description: After the Houston Astros were eliminated from the postseason Wednesday, Alex Bregman posted what might be a farewell after his possible last game with the club.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dolphins' Tyreek Hill was 'motivating' team during sideline outburst in loss to Titans: 'Wasn't frustrated'
item.description: Things were not quite as they may have seemed during an animated moment on the Miami Dolphins sideline, Tyreek Hill clarified on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers to start Jack Flaherty in Game 1 of NLDS, with Yoshinobu Yamamoto set for Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jack Flaherty will start Game 1 of this weekend’s National League Division Series for the Los Angeles Dodgers, with rookie Yoshinobu Yamamoto set for Game 2.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jets' Lazard expects NFL to fine him over gun-like celebration
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (AP) — New York Jets wide receiver Allen Lazard expects to be fined by the NFL for his celebration after a catch against Denver last Sunday that included what officials deemed to be a gun-like gesture. “It’s something that’s very silly,” he said Wednesday. “Very, very silly.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zach LaVine still a Chicago Bull (for now), says he's ready to do whatever teams asks of him this season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Zach LaVine insisted he is healthy and ready to do whatever the Chicago Bulls ask of him this season. Above all, he's ready to put the drama in the past.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz new additions bring experience and leadership
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz did not land the "big game" Danny Ainge talked about in April, but they did sign a few key veteran role players this summer. Patty Mills is about to begin his 16th NBA season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sitake wants to keep BYU hungry during bye week
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off a 34-28 win at Baylor, #17 BYU is 5-0 for the first time in three years. But head coach Kalani Sitake is going to make sure his team keeps its underdog mentality during the bye week. "That's our job," Sitake said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Column: Lightning and thunder could bode well for Bears' offense
item.description: The Chicago Bears have developed a formidable 1-2 punch in running backs D'Andre Swift and Roschon Johnson, who have shown the ability to both smash and slice through defenses, but the success of the offense depends on the performance of the offensive line.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Reports: Pete Rose, baseball's all-time hits leader, dead at 83
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Pete Rose, one of Major League Baseball's most polarizing athletes and the game's all-time hits leader, has died at the age of 83, according to TMZ. Rose's agent, Ryan Fiterman, confirmed the news to TMZ and said, "the family is asking for privacy at this time.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Broncos def. Jets on the road 10-9
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Week 4 of the NFL season saw the Cincinnati Bengals claim their first victory of the year with a 34-24 win. Wide receiver Ja'Marr Chase showcased his speed and balance, sprinting 63 yards for a touchdown.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Americans get biggest road win and capture the Presidents Cup for the 10th straight time
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — U.S. captain Jim Furyk raised the gold trophy before a team that dressed in red shirts that filled the scoreboard early with red scores. The Presidents Cup is one trophy the Americans own, for two decades and counting.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fred Warner's pick-6 helps the 49ers snap a 2-game skid with a 30-13 win over the Patriots
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Even after all the success the San Francisco 49ers had the past few seasons, a two-game skid and a string of injuries early in the season can raise some doubts for any team. Considered those answered for one day at least.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Chastain passes Truex on late restart to win NASCAR Cup Series race at Kansas Speedway
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Ross Chastain finally got to smash a watermelon for the first time this season Sunday at Kansas Speedway. Smashed the hopes of a bunch of playoff drivers trying to earn an automatic berth in the next round, too.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: More brain injuries will occur from contact sports: Doctor
item.description: Brett Favre's diagnosis with Parkinson's disease has brought discussions of the long-term effects of contact sports on brain function to the fore.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: White Sox beat playoff-bound Tigers 4-0, stay at 121 losses going into finale
item.description: Sean Burke pitched two-hit ball over five innings, Andrew Benintendi tied his career high with his 20th homer and the Chicago White Sox beat the playoff-bound Detroit Tigers 4-0 on Saturday to stay at 121 losses going into their season finale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Shohei Ohtani heads to 1st postseason after Dodgers clinch NL West title with a 7-2 win over Padres
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is headed to the postseason for the first time in his career after the Los Angeles Dodgers won the NL West on Thursday night by scoring five runs in the seventh inning in a 7-2 win over the second-place San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: It's official: This Los Angeles Angels team is the worst in franchise history
item.description: The Los Angeles Angels, in their 64th season in franchise history, made history on Thursday — just not the good kind.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: RSL pushes for home playoff advantage in crucial showdown with Austin FC
item.description: Having clinched their playoff spot last weekend, RSL wants a top-four finish and the accompanying home-field advantage in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, Royal Riot Podcast, ABC 4
